{{Youmay|the [[starshipwright]]|[[Vinda (Zabrak)|Vinda]], the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]]}}
{{Quote|Now, the central issue, Captain Solo—please stop shushing me, sir; I will be heard! At issue are two thousand five hundred credits owed Vinda and D'rag, Starshipwrights. Unless you're prepared to make payment, I am empowered to attach and take possession of your ship, which, by the way, appears to have had her marking altered in illegal fashion.|Odumin, to Han Solo|<ref name="hsr" />}}
'''Vinda''' was a [[starshipwright]] and the co-owner of [[Starshipwrights and Aerospace Engineers Incorporated]], a corporation based on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Oslumpex V/Legends|Oslumpex V]], along with his partner [[D'rag]]. In [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] owed them 2,500 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for their work on his [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. Taking advantage of this knowledge, the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]] executive [[Odumin]]—posing as a [[Skip tracer/Legends|skip tracer]] called "Spray," looking to repossess the vessel for Vinda and D'rag—got close to Solo and his [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] partner [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] in his investigation of a [[Slavery/Legends|slaving]] ring in the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]].<ref name="hsr" />

==Behind the scenes==
Vinda was first mentioned in ''[[Han Solo's Revenge]]'', a novel written by [[Brian Daley]] and released in [[1979]].<ref name="hsr" />
